task: Write a short Telegram post on the pain-to-relief pillar: the owner adds up kassa, sklad and the debt notebook separately every evening and it still does not reconcile, then BirLiy quietly brings it into one app on the phone. One pillar, one idea, one CTA.

UZ native mirror of the pain-to-relief Telegram post: kechqurun kassa, ombor va qarz daftari alohida qo'shiladi, baribir to'g'ri kelmaydi; BirLiy hammasini telefondagi bitta ilovaga yig'adi. RU and UZ must carry equivalent meaning.

Write an SEO blog article in RU on replacing the paper debt notebook (qarz daftari) with the in-app nasiya ledger: name the pain in the reader's words, show how BirLiy keeps who owes how much and when they promised to pay, include a short FAQ, end with one CTA. Do not invent a per-customer debt limit (blog-only, needs confirmation).

Write an SEO blog article in UZ on selling without a separate cash register (kassa apparatsiz savdo): a phone is enough to start, no computer required, optional scanner; weave in verified capabilities only and end with one CTA.

Write a product announcement in RU for the verified offline capability: when the internet drops the kassa keeps working, sales are saved locally and sync automatically when the connection returns. State the early-access stage honestly, do not imply a live customer base.

Write a product announcement in UZ for the verified payment-methods capability: naqd, karta, QR va nasiya bitta hisobda; QR uchun alohida terminal shart emas. Keep QR settlement as fast, not a guaranteed instant settlement. Never name a bank or PSP.

Write a pain-to-solution post in RU: stock counts in the notebook do not match the shelf and recounting eats the evening; BirLiy updates the balance automatically after each sale and warns when an item runs low. Describe the pain qualitatively, no invented numbers or percentages.

Write a pain-to-solution post in UZ: while the owner searches a product in the notebook a queue builds at the counter; BirLiy makes the sale a quick flow (scan or search, cart, payment, receipt in Telegram). Keep the 15-second figure as illustration, not a guarantee.

Write an illustrative owner story in RU (NOT a testimonial): a generic neighbourhood-shop owner's day, internet drops at midday but the kassa keeps selling, in the evening the owner sees revenue, stock and who was on shift from the phone, the numbers reconcile themselves. Generic owner only, no named person, no real shop.

UZ native mirror of the illustrative owner story (NOT a testimonial): generic uy yonidagi do'kon egasining kuni; egasi do'konni telefondan ko'radi, kechqurun raqamlar o'z-o'zidan to'g'ri keladi. Generic owner only, no named person, equivalent meaning to the RU version.

Write an objection-handling post in RU answering 'it is complicated to set up, I have no computer': honestly say a phone is enough to start, BirLiy helps install, import the catalog and ring the first receipt with the team. Present one-day onboarding as typical, not a hard guarantee; offer to confirm specifics via @birliy_support_bot.

Write an objection-handling post in UZ answering 'what if a cashier cheats while I am not at the counter': each cashier logs in with their own PIN, a full action log keeps what was sold, written off and returned with the cashier name, and a shift log. Do not turn this into a broad data-protection guarantee.

Write a competitor-comparison post in RU comparing a paper notebook (тетрадь) with BirLiy WITHOUT defamation: respect the familiar notebook, state its honest limitation (debts and balances get lost), then show BirLiy keeps register, stock, debts and reports in one place on a phone. Compare only notebook/Excel/1C, never name a POS vendor.

Write a competitor-comparison post in UZ comparing 1C with BirLiy WITHOUT defamation: 1C kichik do'kon uchun murakkab va qimmat, BirLiy telefonda ishlaydi va o'rganishsiz tushunarli. Only factual claims from the comparison fact; respectful tone; compare only notebook/Excel/1C.

Write an offer post in RU on the early-access pricing: first 6 months 49 000 сум a month, then 149 000, full functionality, no hidden payments, nothing charged without consent; frame it as the first-cohort / pilot rate, no fake scarcity. Quote price wording only from the pricing facts.

UZ native mirror of the offer post: birinchi 6 oy oyiga 49 000 so'm, keyin 149 000, to'liq funksionallik, yashirin to'lovlarsiz, roziligingizsiz hech narsa yechib olinmaydi; birinchi kogorta narxi sifatida, soxta tanqislisiz. Equivalent meaning to the RU offer.

Plan a one-week 3-post campaign in RU on the Mon/Wed/Fri rhythm: Monday pain-to-relief (notebook), Wednesday one-feature-big (offline kassa), Friday offer (early-access price). Each slot names pillar, template, fact ids, CTA. No single pillar repeats; no escalating scarcity across days.

Plan a one-week 3-post campaign in UZ on the Dushanba/Chorshanba/Juma rhythm: Monday by-segment (minimarket), Wednesday trust (PIN, action log, made for Uzbekistan), Friday offer (early-access price). Each slot names pillar, template, fact ids, CTA. Equivalent structure to the RU campaign.

Write a one-feature-big post in RU on the offline capability: one feature, one idea, calm tone; the internet drops, the kassa keeps working, sales save locally and sync when the connection returns. End with one CTA.

Write a by-segment post in RU for a pharmacy: precise per-item accounting and stock balances on a phone, payments in one place. Use verified capabilities only; do not over-promise expiry features beyond catalog/stock. End with one CTA.

Write a by-segment post in UZ for a service point (repair, dry cleaning, tailoring): take payment and keep an order log in one app on the phone; verified capabilities only, no hospitality over-promises. End with one CTA.

Write a capability-led case study in RU framed as 'a shop like yours' (NOT a real customer): a generic minimarket's manual before-state described qualitatively, then the BirLiy workflow step by step tied to verified facts, what changes for the owner in plain terms, honest early-access scope. ZERO results metrics of any kind.

Write an SEO content brief in UZ for an article on stock accounting in a small shop (skladskoy uchet / ombor hisobi): lock the keyword, intent, H2/H3 outline tagged with fact ids, 2-4 FAQ questions, an allowed-facts list and a do-not-use list naming the prohibited demo facts, internal links and the single CTA. Mark competitor mentions as notebook/Excel/1C only.

Write a trust-pillar post in RU on 'Сделано для Узбекистана': a system built for local business, not a faceless cloud, with PIN login and a full action log nearby. NEVER expand the made-for-Uzbekistan line to name a bank or parent company. Do not overstate security beyond PIN, roles and the action log.

Write an objection-handling post in UZ answering 'do I need a payment terminal for QR': no, the buyer scans the QR code on the phone screen; cash, card, QR and nasiya are tracked in one place. Present settlement as fast, not a guaranteed instant settlement, and never name a bank or PSP. Offer to confirm specifics via @birliy_support_bot.
